# Django Debug Toolbar {{ latest-version }}

{{ jazzband }} {{ build-status }} {{ coverage }} {{ docs }} {{ python-support }} {{ django-support }}

The Django Debug Toolbar is a configurable set of panels that display various
debug information about the current request/response and when clicked, display
more details about the panel's content.

Here's a screenshot of the toolbar in action:

```{image} https://raw.github.com/jazzband/django-debug-toolbar/main/example/django-debug-toolbar.png
:alt: Django Debug Toolbar screenshot
```

In addition to the built-in panels, a number of third-party panels are
contributed by the community.

The current stable version of the Debug Toolbar is 4.4.6. It works on
Django ≥ 4.2.0.

The Debug Toolbar does not currently support [Django's asynchronous views](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/topics/async/).

Documentation, including installation and configuration instructions, is
available at <https://django-debug-toolbar.readthedocs.io/>.

The Django Debug Toolbar is released under the BSD license, like Django
itself. If you like it, please consider contributing!

The Django Debug Toolbar was originally created by Rob Hudson \<<mailto:rob@cogit8.org>>
in August 2008 and was further developed by many [contributors].

[contributors]: https://github.com/jazzband/django-debug-toolbar/graphs/contributors

classes.name: debug_toolbar.panels.sql.forms.SQLSelectForm
documentation:
Validate params

    sql: The sql statement with interpolated params
    raw_sql: The sql statement with placeholders
    params: JSON encoded parameter values
    duration: time for SQL to execute passed in from toolbar just for redisplay

functions.name: debug_toolbar.utils.tidy_stacktrace
documentation:
Clean up stacktrace and remove all entries that are excluded by the
HIDE_IN_STACKTRACES setting.

`stack` should be a list of frame tuples from `inspect.stack()` or
`debug_toolbar.utils.get_stack()`.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.utils.get_name_from_obj
documentation: Get the best name as `str` from a view or a object.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.utils.getframeinfo
documentation:
Get information about a frame or traceback object.

A tuple of five things is returned: the filename, the line number of
the current line, the function name, a list of lines of context from
the source code, and the index of the current line within that list.
The optional second argument specifies the number of lines of context
to return, which are centered around the current line.

This originally comes from `inspect` but is modified to handle issues
with `findsource()`.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.utils.get_sorted_request_variable
documentation:
Get a data structure for showing a sorted list of variables from the
request data.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.utils.get_stack
documentation:
Get a list of records for a frame and all higher (calling) frames.

Each record contains a frame object, filename, line number, function
name, a list of lines of context, and index within the context.

Modified version of `inspect.stack()` which calls our own `getframeinfo()`

functions.name: debug_toolbar.utils.get_stack_trace
documentation:
Return a processed stack trace for the current call stack.

If the `ENABLE_STACKTRACES` setting is False, return an empty {class}`list`.
Otherwise return a {class}`list` of processed stack frame tuples (file name, line
number, function name, source line, frame locals) for the current call stack.  The
first entry in the list will be for the bottom of the stack and the last entry will
be for the top of the stack.

`skip` is an {class}`int` indicating the number of stack frames above the frame
for this function to omit from the stack trace.  The default value of `0` means
that the entry for the caller of this function will be the last entry in the
returned stack trace.

Check if the function is from the project code.

Project code is identified by the BASE_DIR setting

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.cache.CachePanel.current_instance
documentation:
Return the currently enabled CachePanel instance or None.

If a request is in process with a CachePanel enabled, this will return that
panel (based on the current thread or async task).  Otherwise it will return
None.
Panel that displays the cache statistics.

classes.name: debug_toolbar.forms.SignedDataForm
documentation:
Helper form that wraps a form to validate its contents on post.

class PanelForm(forms.Form):
    # fields

On render:
    form = SignedDataForm(initial=PanelForm(initial=data).initial)

On POST:
    signed_form = SignedDataForm(request.POST)
    if signed_form.is_valid():
        panel_form = PanelForm(signed_form.verified_data)
        if panel_form.is_valid():
            # Success

functions.name: debug_toolbar.settings.update_toolbar_config
documentation: Refresh configuration when overriding settings.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.apps.check_template_config
documentation:
Checks if a template configuration is valid.

The toolbar requires either the toolbars to be unspecified or
`django.template.loaders.app_directories.Loader` to be
included in the loaders.
If custom loaders are specified, then APP_DIRS must be True.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.apps.check_panel_configs
documentation: Allow each panel to check the toolbar's integration for their its own purposes.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.apps.js_mimetype_check
documentation: Check that JavaScript files are resolving to the correct content type.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.apps.debug_toolbar_installed_when_running_tests_check
documentation: Check that the toolbar is not being used when tests are running

classes.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.panel_id
documentation: Private panel properties

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.nav_title
documentation: Title shown in the side bar. Defaults to {attr}`title`.
Titles and content

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.nav_subtitle
documentation: Subtitle shown in the side bar. Defaults to the empty string.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.has_content
documentation:
`True` if the panel can be displayed in full screen, `False` if
it's only shown in the side bar. Defaults to `True`.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.is_historical
documentation:
Panel supports rendering historical values.

Defaults to {attr}`has_content`.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.title
documentation:
Title shown in the panel when it's displayed in full screen.

Mandatory, unless the panel sets {attr}`has_content` to `False`.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.template
documentation:
Template used to render {attr}`content`.

Mandatory, unless the panel sets {attr}`has_content` to `False` or
overrides {attr}`content`.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.content
documentation:
Content of the panel when it's displayed in full screen.

By default this renders the template defined by {attr}`template`.
Statistics stored with {meth}`record_stats` are available in the
template's context.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.scripts
documentation:
Scripts used by the HTML content of the panel when it's displayed.

When a panel is rendered on the frontend, the `djdt.panel.render`
JavaScript event will be dispatched. The scripts can listen for
this event to support dynamic functionality.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.ready
documentation:
Perform early initialization for the panel.

This should only include initialization or instrumentation that needs to
be done unconditionally for the panel regardless of whether it is
enabled for a particular request.  It should be idempotent.
Panel early initialization

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.get_urls
documentation: Return URLpatterns, if the panel has its own views.
URLs for panel-specific views

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.enable_instrumentation
documentation:
Enable instrumentation to gather data for this panel.

This usually means monkey-patching (!) or registering signal
receivers. Any instrumentation with a non-negligible effect on
performance should be installed by this method rather than at import
time.

Unless the toolbar or this panel is disabled, this method will be
called early in `DebugToolbarMiddleware`. It should be idempotent.
Enable and disable (expensive) instrumentation, must be idempotent

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.disable_instrumentation
documentation:
Disable instrumentation to gather data for this panel.

This is the opposite of {meth}`enable_instrumentation`.

Unless the toolbar or this panel is disabled, this method will be
called late in the middleware. It should be idempotent.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.record_stats
documentation:
Store data gathered by the panel. `stats` is a {class}`dict`.

Each call to `record_stats` updates the statistics dictionary.
Store and retrieve stats (shared between panels for no good reason)

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.get_stats
documentation: Access data stored by the panel. Returns a {class}`dict`.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.record_server_timing
documentation:
Store data gathered by the panel. `stats` is a {class}`dict`.

Each call to `record_stats` updates the statistics dictionary.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.get_server_timing_stats
documentation: Access data stored by the panel. Returns a {class}`dict`.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.process_request
documentation:
Like \_\_call\_\_ in Django's middleware.

Write panel logic related to the request there. Save data with
{meth}`record_stats`.

Return the existing response or overwrite it.
Standard middleware methods

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.get_headers
documentation:
Get headers the panel needs to set.

Called after {meth}`process_request
<debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.generate_stats>` and
{meth}`process_request<debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.generate_stats>`

Header values will be appended if multiple panels need to set it.

By default it sets the Server-Timing header.

Return dict of headers to be appended.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.generate_stats
documentation:
Write panel logic related to the response there. Post-process data
gathered while the view executed. Save data with {meth}`record_stats`.

Called after {meth}`process_request
<debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.process_request>`.

Does not return a value.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.generate_server_timing
documentation:
Similar to {meth}`generate_stats
<debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.generate_stats>`,

Generate stats for Server Timing <https://w3c.github.io/server-timing/>

Does not return a value.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.panels.Panel.run_checks
documentation:
Check that the integration is configured correctly for the panel.

This will be called as a part of the Django checks system when the
application is being setup.

Return a list of {class}`django.core.checks.CheckMessage` instances.
Base class for panels.

modules.name: debug_toolbar.middleware
documentation: Debug Toolbar middleware

functions.name: debug_toolbar.middleware.show_toolbar
documentation: Default function to determine whether to show the toolbar on a given page.

classes.name: debug_toolbar.middleware.DebugToolbarMiddleware

Middleware to set up Debug Toolbar on incoming request and render toolbar

functions.name: debug_toolbar.decorators.render_with_toolbar_language
documentation: Force any rendering within the view to use the toolbar's language.

modules.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar
documentation: The main DebugToolbar class that loads and renders the Toolbar.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.observe_request
documentation: Determine whether to update the toolbar from a client side request.

functions.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.debug_toolbar_urls
documentation:
Return a URL pattern for serving toolbar in debug mode.

from django.conf import settings
from debug_toolbar.toolbar import debug_toolbar_urls

urlpatterns = [
    # ... the rest of your URLconf goes here ...
] + debug_toolbar_urls()

methods.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.DebugToolbar.panels
documentation: Get a list of all available panels.
Manage panels

methods.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.DebugToolbar.enabled_panels
documentation: Get a list of panels enabled for the current request.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.DebugToolbar.get_panel_by_id
documentation: Get the panel with the given id, which is the class name by default.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.DebugToolbar.render_toolbar
documentation: Renders the overall Toolbar with panels inside.
Handle rendering the toolbar in HTML

methods.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.DebugToolbar.should_render_panels
documentation:
Determine whether the panels should be rendered during the request

If False, the panels will be loaded via Ajax.

methods.name: debug_toolbar.toolbar.DebugToolbar.is_toolbar_request
documentation: Determine if the request is for a DebugToolbar view.
